description: Academics, researchers, scribes, and learned professionals.
jobs: Scribe
An ink-stained copyist filling page after page with neat script by quill and candlelight.
Tutor
A patient instructor teaching a noble's children letters, sums, and manners in private lessons.
Illuminator
A gold-leaf-laying artist painting tiny figures and colored borders into manuscript margins.
Scholar
A book-piled thinker reading ancient texts and writing treatises in a cluttered study.
Philosopher
A deep-browed ponderer of truth, existence, and virtue, filling pages with long arguments.
Astronomer
A night-sky watcher tracking planets and stars with brass instruments and inked charts.
Astrologer
A star-gazing mystic charting horoscopes and advising nobles on the turns of fate.
Natural Philosopher
A specimen-collecting observer seeking the laws behind plants, minerals, and weather.
Mathematician
A chalk-dusted prover working through calculations and solving problems of geometry and number.
Apothecary
A grimy apothecary grinding herbs and minerals into salves, tonics, and dubious cure-alls.
Veterinarian
A stable-kneeling healer treating sick horses and lame cattle with herbs and poultices.
Teacher
A chalk-handed instructor standing before rows of children, drilling reading and arithmetic.
Professor
A lectern-gripping academic examining students and delivering hall-filling talks on their subject.
Lecturer
A podium-standing speaker delivering formal talks on law, medicine, or theology.
Schoolmaster
A switch-carrying taskmaster running a village schoolroom and drilling children in their letters.
Grammar Master
A syntax-obsessed driller of Latin grammar, correcting students until they get it right.
Librarian
A volume-cataloging guardian of rare holdings, shelving manuscripts in careful order.
Archivist
A vault-dwelling preserver of deeds, treaties, and crumbling records under lock and key.
Cartographer
A fine-penned drawer of coastlines, roads, and mountain ranges onto parchment maps.
Surveyor
A chain-stretching measurer of land boundaries, planting stakes across open fields.
Architect
A compass-and-ruler designer of castles and cathedrals, overseeing their construction.
Translator
A multilingual converter of written texts, preserving meaning across languages.
Interpreter
A quick-tongued voice standing between two speakers, bridging languages in real time.
Copyist
A page-by-page reproducer of manuscripts, spreading knowledge to distant libraries.
Spell Scribe
A precision-obsessed copier of arcane formulas into spellbooks, where one wrong stroke means disaster.
Magical Scholar
A theory-testing student of magic through experiments, texts, and careful observation.
Sage
A decades-deep well of knowledge answering difficult questions others cannot solve.
Mentor
A wing-taking guide shaping a younger person's skills and character over patient years.
Life Coach
A question-asking listener guiding others toward clearer decisions about their path.
